challenges.title: Paragraph Basics
challenges.description:
Create three

paragraphs about any topic you like. Each paragraph should contain at least a few words of text. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Write your three paragraphs here -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert html.lower().count('

') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three

elements' assert html.lower().count('

') >= 3, 'Should close all three

elements' paras = html.lower().split('

')[1:] for i, p in enumerate(paras[:3]): text = p.split('

')[0].strip() assert len(text) > 0, f'Paragraph {i+1} should contain some text'
hints:
Each paragraph is wrapped in

...

tags
Write any text you want inside each paragraph — the test just checks structure

challenges.title: Preformatted Text
challenges.description:
Create a

paragraph with a sentence, then a 

 element containing some formatted text or code (with indentation or line breaks preserved). Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Write a paragraph and a pre element here -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a

paragraph element' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

element' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a 
 element for preformatted text'
assert '
' in html.lower(), 'Should close the 
 element'
pre_content = html.lower().split('
')[1].split('
')[0]
assert len(pre_content.strip()) > 0, 'The 
 element should contain some text'
hints: The <pre> element preserves whitespace and line breaks exactly as written
Use <pre> when you want to display code or text with specific formatting
